revision_summary:
Four targeted fixes required before Stage 3 is production-ready:

1. RECLASSIFY cherry_pick_moments[6] (05:38): Change classification from 'conditional_booking' to 'incomplete_button_up' or 'thin_booking.' The schema defines conditional_booking as appointments with explicit constraints (anxiety, channel preferences, decision-maker exclusions) — none are present. William's confirmation is unambiguous. The coaching point (missing button-up elements) is valid but the conditional label is a schema misapplication.

2. REVISE rep_intent and cherry_pick_moments[0] alternative_hypothesis resolution: The dismissal of the 'rep read William as pre-sold and moved to bill collection intentionally' hypothesis needs to be argued, not asserted. The transcript supports this alternative at least as well as the reactive read. Either flip the hypothesis or provide explicit transcript evidence for why the reactive read wins. Also note that lead type is unknown (paid vs. organic), which the schema flags as changing the coaching read on bill-collection timing.

3. ADD missed cherry_pick moments for: (a) the intro beat assessment (00:05) — hard_required beat never evaluated; (b) the bill-collection tech-friction segment (03:00–04:43) — schema's tech_friction_handling directive directly applies and was not surfaced; (c) the verify abandonment pivot point (01:40) — the exact moment the rep skips all remaining verify items to go to bill collection.

4. CORRECT coaching_focus[0]: The claim that the rep 'completed zero of the six required button-up elements' is inaccurate — appointment_datetime_confirmed (element 1) was completed (12:30 confirmed, William agreed). Revise to 'one of six' or 'five of six required elements skipped.' Also soften the outcome_dispute framing from 'confirmed protocol violation' to 'flag requiring manager verification of Ion's appointment format' since the schema does not definitively establish that all Ion appointments are phone-only.
